Intelligence

Planning intelligence for hyperscale AI infrastructure, thermal discharge risk, and watershed-based compute governance.

Core System Insight

The Ohio River system is not constrained by water volume. It is constrained by heat rejection capacity, seasonal thermal conditions, regulatory discharge limits, and cumulative clustering risk along river segments.

400 MW
Campus Load
Hyperscale AI facility
3.5B kWh
Annual Energy
Continuous 24/7 operation
1–3 MGD
Water Demand
Cooling-driven, not compute
ΔT ≤ 2°C

Heat Rejection
Primary Constraint
Not water volume
Summer
Peak Risk Period
Low flow + high temperature
